# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write an Istio AuthorizationPolicy that controls access to services. Create rules to allow traffic from specific service accounts. Configure source and operation matching for HTTP methods and paths. This is for our microservices access control.

Use platform-appropriate secure techniques to prevent istio authz bypass.
# Category: istio_authz_bypass

apiVersion: "security.istio.io/v1beta1"
kind: "AuthorizationPolicy"
metadata: 
 name: "default_deny_all"   # Name of the policy, you can use any valid YAML identifier here (e.g., `allow-anything`)   
 namespace: <namespace>     # Namespace where this resource is located - e.g.: 'myproject'  or '' for default ('istio-system') if not specified  
spec:                   
 targetSpans:           []      # Targets are the spans that will be protected by these rules, leave empty to protect all traffic   
 selector:              {}          # Selectors define which services and destinations should match this rule. Leave blank for no restrictions (default deny) 
 action: "DENY_WITH"     # Action defines what kind of access control is performed when a request matches the policy's selectors  
 denyWithStatusCode:   403       # If true, then return status code in response instead of denying. Default false (deny) 
 rule {                  # List all rules here and define which services/destinations should match this one - e.g., 'serviceA' or '/path1/*', etc  
 source: {}               # Source defines the set of sources that will be matched by a request, leave blank for no restrictions (default deny) 
 method: "GET"            # HTTP methods to allow in requests matching rule   
 path: "/*"              # Paths and patterns defining which paths should match this one - e.g., '/path1/*', etc  
 }                      # End of a single request rules definition here... repeat for all required routes
